Orryn Dasch.name: Orryn Dasch
Orryn Dasch.basicInfo: Fifteen years fighting the Threshi winter-raid cycle on the Kelmari marches; reads incoming raid-lines by instinct. Resigned his post after an order he couldn't bring himself to execute. Now hires east-facing, always — anything that puts him moving toward the marches.
personality: blunt to the point of insulting
dry humor that lands harder than he intends
drinks too much and sings off-key
refuses any job involving children
compulsive about paying for rounds he didn't pay for last time

Mira Coyle.name: Mira Coyle
Mira Coyle.basicInfo: Volunteered into the marches at sixteen and ran the long border patrols on foot for a decade — javelins, hatchet, light mail, the runner's kit. Was passed over for command for reasons the unit did not state but everyone understood. Now hires individually at high rates and refers no one.
personality: proud
hot-tempered and quick to laugh
vain about her kit
holds a grudge by lineage
will not refuse a duel and is famous for it
intensely loyal once she decides someone is worth it

Desten Ulvar.name: Desten Ulvar
Desten Ulvar.basicInfo: Knows the Whitestone fen-country better than any man alive. Takes only jobs that keep him within a day of water; refuses anything in the high country flat. Excellent cook over a fire and known across three road-houses for it.
personality: cheerful gambler off the clock
careless with money
has a wife in two villages and pretends each does not exist
excellent storyteller about his own bad decisions
absurdly competitive at tavern games

Senna Vrole.name: Senna Vrole
Senna Vrole.basicInfo: Ten years on the long roads after a decade on the lake. Carries a yew bow and six knives. Has been saving for a boat for six years and the price keeps somehow staying ahead of her purse.
personality: devout — touches the Hand-sigil before everything
sentimental drunk who recites her grandmother's lake-songs at length
sharp-eyed about people but trusts them anyway
easily offended by tavern food
cries quietly at sad ballads

Perris Adol.name: Perris Adol
Perris Adol.basicInfo: Was in the Avenor Palace Guard until last year; knows the layout of every major building in the city. Why he was removed has several different answers depending on who you ask. Hires now to whoever can afford the rates and the discretion.
personality: bitter wit
name-drops palace gossip incessantly
charming when sober
petty thief of small valuable things
loyal to whoever signs the contract — exactly until the contract ends

Tena Harl.name: Tena Harl
Tena Harl.basicInfo: Queen's College third-year, walked out before the final examinations. Carries a full reservoir and the calm of someone who knows she is the most dangerous person in most rooms. Takes jobs that need both the physical and magical end handled.
personality: loud
flirts indiscriminately and competently
holds grudges with style
generous with coin and unsolicited opinions
openly contemptuous of the Brotherhood and lets them know

Colveth the Rope.name: Colveth the Rope
Colveth the Rope.basicInfo: Extraction specialist — gets people out of places they should not have entered. Does not ask how they got there; asks what they are worth. Owns three identical knives because he keeps losing one a year.
personality: methodical
cheerful in a way that is faintly worrying
takes notes during conversations
strict about meal times
honest about his rates and shameless about them

Dorvann Far-Running.name: Dorvann Far-Running
Dorvann Far-Running.basicInfo: Clan lost standing in a contract dispute three years ago; he has been working road contracts since, paying the debt home. Carries a bow slightly too good for a simple debtor. Sends most of his pay home and lives lean by choice.
personality: diligent
homesick and not embarrassed about it
plays a three-string instrument badly and often
wins at cards and pretends he does not
writes long letters home that get longer the further he is from finishing the debt

Kelrath of the Short Horn.name: Kelrath of the Short Horn
Kelrath of the Short Horn.basicInfo: Thirty years as a caravan guard before a raid wiped the caravan. Refuses caravan work now; hires as a bodyguard. The broken mane-horn dates from the raid; the name was given to him at the hiring markets and stuck.
personality: garrulous
recycles three war stories with growing embellishment
softens up clients with chatter so they sign before they notice
affectionate uncle to half the road-houses
remembers every road-house keeper's children's names

Isavara.name: Isavara
Isavara.basicInfo: Cast out by her clan for a breach of contract she maintains was not her fault — the full story takes an evening to hear. Takes assassination work that no clan-bound Hofnar would accept. Charges fairly and delivers cleanly.
personality: sardonic
eats voraciously
openly lonely
will not lie even when lying would help her
loyal beyond reason to anyone who stands by her once

Ssareth of the Pale Flank.name: Ssareth of the Pale Flank
Ssareth of the Pale Flank.basicInfo: Sixty years of foreign contract work have made him the most experienced Draklid mercenary currently working. Very expensive, extremely precise, one contract at a time, completed exactly as written.
personality: cultured
vain about his scales and grooming
plays a Draklid board game called threshold and will teach anyone who asks
recites poetry three centuries old
charges enough that no fool ever hires him

Vorreth clan-unbound.name: Vorreth clan-unbound
Vorreth clan-unbound.basicInfo: Disowned five years ago; the clan sigil is gone from his belt. Works with a calm that reads as indifference but is complete focus. The reason for the disowning involves a foreign noble who is now dead.
personality: wry
bitter humor about ceremony and clan
surprisingly tender to children and stray animals
refuses to wear gold of any kind
cooks elaborate meals at camp and shares them

Karreth dau-Issath.name: Karreth dau-Issath
Karreth dau-Issath.basicInfo: A rare female working abroad without a clan mandate. Carries her birth clan's colors but has no male relative backing her posting. Takes contracts requiring precision over force.
personality: direct
intense in conversation
drinks bitter coffee at every meeting and offers it to clients who hate it
collects maps
has opinions about every regional cuisine and shares them unprompted

Thresh of the Long Oath.name: Thresh of the Long Oath
Thresh of the Long Oath.basicInfo: Currently fifty years into a forty-year oath he extended because the principal's heir needed protecting. Has thoughts about this. Writes long letters to a sister who has stopped replying.
personality: resigned
dryly funny in long delayed asides
deeply attached to the principal's family in ways that complicate his work
increasingly reckless about his own safety
a careful gardener of the principal's small kitchen-yard

Irrath the Rewritten.name: Irrath the Rewritten
Irrath the Rewritten.basicInfo: First posting, recently sworn, formally correct to the point of being unsettling. Still learning what the contract language actually covers. More dangerous than he looks.
personality: eager to please
bookish
awkward around women he respects
memorizes contract language obsessively
has a stutter that disappears entirely in a fight

Var ash Torrath.name: Var ash Torrath
Var ash Torrath.basicInfo: Third season abroad and outlasting nearly every other Fernwarg male his pack has sent out. Mane decorated with four trophies his pack-mother does not yet know about. Getting very good.
personality: cocky
charming with humans in a slightly off-rhythm way
growing in confidence with each contract
privately worried he has started enjoying foreign work too much
composes pack-songs no human will ever hear

Orresh ash Kullen.name: Orresh ash Kullen
Orresh ash Kullen.basicInfo: Sixth season abroad — has been out longer than most Fernwarg males survive. Fights in a style no formal school would recognize, assembled entirely from necessity. Beginning to understand he will not return to the pack.
personality: worn
philosophical
mentors younger Fernwarg abroad whether they want it or not
drinks Hofnar spirits and refuses anything human-distilled
keeps a pet crow he insists is incidental

Vesh ash Mirrath.name: Vesh ash Mirrath
Vesh ash Mirrath.basicInfo: Scout specialist. Has never fought in a straight engagement when he could avoid it; has avoided it every time. Commands premium rates for route-clearing and intelligence work.
personality: witty
sharp-tongued
habitual gambler
tells very tall stories about narrow escapes that turn out, on investigation, to be true
will not eat in groups

Ith-Serrath.name: Ith-Serrath
Ith-Serrath.basicInfo: Has broken with the elders' teaching so thoroughly and for so long that she has stopped feeling the weight of it. Works alone. Will not take contracts against Raknid; everything else is negotiable.
personality: considered
deeply polite
well-read in continental poetry and quotes from it
will discuss any work openly except who paid for it
carries a small embroidered cushion in her travel pack and does not explain it

Vel-Imrak.name: Vel-Imrak
Vel-Imrak.basicInfo: Queen's College-trained witch who supplements his income with discreet contract work. The elders know and pretend not to. He is the best argument they have for Avenorian integration and also the thing they are most quietly worried about.
personality: charismatic
flirtatious in a mock-formal way
ambitious enough that the College senior staff have started watching him
particular about wine and pays for the good kind himself
writes letters in the old Raknid script for fun

Serrha of the Grey Shift.name: Serrha of the Grey Shift
Serrha of the Grey Shift.basicInfo: Works entirely outside House sanction; the Houses maintain plausible ignorance. Operates inland, which makes her unusual — she carries brine flasks and hates every moment of it. Extraordinary at extracting things from secured locations.
personality: caustic humor about every aspect of land life
sentimental about brine and particular about her flask
runs a side business in luxury imports nobody asks about
punctual to a fault
tips generously and remembers every server

Pellat of the Narrow Tide.name: Pellat of the Narrow Tide
Pellat of the Narrow Tide.basicInfo: Former House marine, now freelance. Speaks plainly, which is unusual for his kind and is itself a signal. Takes the contracts that require entering somewhere not designed for a land creature.
personality: gregarious by Cephalen standards
big slow laugh
careful about who he drinks with and very generous with those few
takes apprentices and trains them honestly
hates closed rooms with only one exit

Eckrath the Dissident.name: Eckrath the Dissident
Eckrath the Dissident.basicInfo: One of fewer than a dozen Threshi working under Avenorian writ in the current decade. His sickle-blades and the panic they produce in human levies make him expensive and effective.
personality: studious of human customs and earnest if awkward at them
collects books in five scripts he cannot read
has a human teacher he is half in love with and will not admit it
fastidious about cleaning his chitin
obsessively careful about contract language because he was lied to once

Torrath Sickle-Two.name: Torrath Sickle-Two
Torrath Sickle-Two.basicInfo: Has survived four contract seasons, which no one told him was unusual until he was two seasons in. The 'Two' refers to the replacement sickle-blade an Avenorian smith fitted after the first contract. He is on his fifth.
personality: dry humor
increasingly fluent in three human tongues
sends remittance to a Threshi dissident community he has never visited
particular about which smith fits his replacement blades
warm with strangers and reserved with friends, in that order

Gurdham the Contract-Obliged.name: Gurdham the Contract-Obliged
Gurdham the Contract-Obliged.basicInfo: Took one contract to settle a tavern bet and discovered he was shockingly good at the work. Has not stopped. Always planning the meal that will mark his retirement; the meal grows more elaborate each season.
personality: cheerful and complains constantly, the two not in tension
negotiates in food-allowance as much as coin
carries a recipe book and adds to it after every contract
moves faster than anyone expects
openly enjoys the violence in a way that occasionally unsettles his employers

Rubb Hollow-Roots.name: Rubb Hollow-Roots
Rubb Hollow-Roots.basicInfo: Tunnel-fighting specialist; hired for a single extraction four years ago and has not gone home. Has opinions about surface food that have softened slightly with prolonged exposure.
personality: loud
pragmatic
cheerfully amoral about meat dishes the tunnel-elders would have refused
hosts uncomfortable evenings introducing surface friends to each other
deeply suspicious of dry weather

Harrunn of House Kasseth.name: Harrunn of House Kasseth
Harrunn of House Kasseth.basicInfo: High-value cargo escort specialist. The House sigil at his lapel means the cargo is protected by implicit guarantee of House revenge against any who touch it. This is not a small thing.
personality: methodical
dry humor in long delayed asides
drinks heavily off-shift and never on
reads three Vorok contract treatises in rotation and quotes them at length
courteous to subordinates and brutal to peers in negotiation

Orrath Free-Cast.name: Orrath Free-Cast
Orrath Free-Cast.basicInfo: Operates without House backing, which means the work will not appear on a House manifest. Clients understand this. Knows everyone worth knowing in the back-country and is owed favors by half of them.
personality: cynical
charming when the contract requires it and not otherwise
carries a bird whistle and uses it for signals
remembers every favor in both directions
fond of a particular Hofnar trader he has been quietly courting for two years

Old Grunt.name: Old Grunt
Old Grunt.basicInfo: Has survived enough hunts to develop something approaching cunning. Picks distinct ambush spots by season and gores hunters who carry the weapon-style of the one whose spearhead is still in his flank. The local Fernwarg pack-mother has quietly asked that he not be killed.
personality: patient
vindictive against specific weapon-types
territorial about a particular wallow
will not engage parties of more than three
occasionally seen following children at distance and not attacking

The Tolling Bridge.name: The Tolling Bridge
The Tolling Bridge.basicInfo: Holds his Whitestone-country river crossing on fixed terms for forty years: one toll, one meal, guaranteed passage. Has eaten two parties in living memory; local consensus says they deserved it both times.
personality: genial in negotiation
fair to repeat travelers and gives small discounts to children
remembers every face that has crossed
tells long unfunny jokes and laughs at them himself
deeply fond of cured river-eel and accepts it in lieu of toll

Keth the Two-Voiced.name: Keth the Two-Voiced
Keth the Two-Voiced.basicInfo: Two heads. The day-head bargains in plain trade-tongue; the night-head sings in a language no scholar has identified. Trades passage for news of the world below. Has favorites among regular travelers and asks after their families.
personality: the day-head is genial and slow
the night-head is melancholy and curious
they argue with each other in front of guests
collects small painted stones offered by travelers
rumored to be writing — slowly — a history of the pass

The Foreman.name: The Foreman
The Foreman.basicInfo: Runs a toll-road stretch of the Reach interior. Pay in labor, livestock, or information; refuse and you go to the den. Has been known to give accurate directions to the parties he taxes.
personality: business-like
proud of the den and gives tours to those who survive the first day
obsessively organized about his bone-pile
reads aloud from a stolen ledger he does not understand and pretends to
open to renegotiation if the offer is properly framed

Sorrow-in-the-Wood.name: Sorrow-in-the-Wood
Sorrow-in-the-Wood.basicInfo: Has a wider orchard territory than most skellach and the habit of releasing captives with a small carved piece of wood. The carvings are recognizable to other skellach and confer some kind of safe passage. Some of the released captives come back voluntarily, with offerings.
personality: slow rural cadence
philosophical about his appetites
boastful about his orchard
sentimental about returning visitors
trades fruit and rope-work for news of the wider world

Garrat the Loud.name: Garrat the Loud
Garrat the Loud.basicInfo: Half of a twenty-year extortion partnership operating out of an abandoned foundry near Avenor's backwoods. Negotiates loudly and badly while his partner Gillen watches the exits. Sentimental about a particular human innkeeper who feeds them in winter.
personality: loudmouth
swears constantly and creatively
fundamentally lazy
haggles over copper coins with theatrical fury
weeps openly at sad songs

Gillen the Silent.name: Gillen the Silent
Gillen the Silent.basicInfo: Half of the foundry partnership; watches the exits while Garrat negotiates. Speaks one or two sentences a year, by Garrat's count, all of them on the day they were robbed by an ulk and recovered the hoard.
personality: calm
patient
strategic where Garrat is impulsive
obsessive about polishing the iron weapon
secretly the household's bookkeeper and corrects Garrat's math when alone

Kilward the Half-Husk.name: Kilward the Half-Husk
Kilward the Half-Husk.basicInfo: Controls a ruined frontier fort and holds court in the old hall. Has kept the Wartide things in his territory from raiding three nearby villages for eleven years, in exchange for an annual tribute. Running a long game involving the Primes that no one has yet identified.
personality: formal in old-soldier register
receives properly-arrived emissaries with full hospitality
openly contemptuous of newer Bloodthanes
collects pre-Wartide books and reads them at length
the half-husk part of the name was never explained and he discourages questions

Graunt Blade-Behind.name: Graunt Blade-Behind
Graunt Blade-Behind.basicInfo: Commands a band of five on the Long Steppe margin. Honors parleys, accepts road-tolls in livestock, holds grudges by lineage. The back-camp captives keep escaping; he has begun to suspect one of his own band is letting them go.
personality: formal in the old-soldiery cadence
vain about his armor
tracks rivalries within his own band obsessively
surprisingly knowledgeable about Long Steppe weather
increasingly paranoid about the suspected traitor

The Maulerbear of the Third Mile.name: The Maulerbear of the Third Mile
The Maulerbear of the Third Mile.basicInfo: Has held a stretch of Reach interior for thirty years. Marks territory by tearing trail-signs and re-carving them with claw-script. Has a known habit of pacing alongside parties for a full mile before deciding whether to engage.
personality: taunts in slow rumbling common-tongue
names what it intends to do before doing it
particular about the carving of its own signs
remembers parties that have escaped and recognizes them years later
occasionally lets one party through for reasons no one has worked out

Pell the Charming.name: Pell the Charming
Pell the Charming.basicInfo: Refused twice in a single season, evolved into something closer to an information broker on the Hollows fringes. Information accurate; small print binds. Has clients across three kingdoms who have learned not to ask too directly where she gets what she knows.
personality: theatrical hospitality
serves tea she does not poison
openly fond of gossip and pays for the good kind
maintains a regular Fernwarg supplier she considers a friend
sentimental about old letters and keeps a drawer of them

Old Skeg.name: Old Skeg
Old Skeg.basicInfo: Holds a deep Reach river crossing that may have existed before the Ensoulment. Asks a question of every traveler instead of charging a toll; pass if you answer to his satisfaction. The questions are different every time and not always serious.
personality: riddle-fond
tells jokes badly and accepts jokes told back
knows old continental gossip from before any current kingdom
vain about his collection of polished river-stones
occasionally simply tired and waves parties through with no question

The Kelker of the Old Questions.name: The Kelker of the Old Questions
The Kelker of the Old Questions.basicInfo: Holds the deep Impasse and has done since before any settlement now standing was founded. Asks the same handful of questions across centuries — all of them about what the world was before the Ensoulment. Three answerers in recorded history have left unharmed.
personality: patient on a scale that breaks human understanding
delivers the questions as if for the first time, every time
fascinated by small concrete details of contemporary life
trades occasionally for offerings of preserved fruit
openly disappointed by wrong answers but never angry

The Loam-Drinker of the Abandoned Galler.name: The Loam-Drinker of the Abandoned Gallery
The Loam-Drinker of the Abandoned Galler.basicInfo: Spoke twice in a hundred years: a warning that saved sixty lives, and an untranslatable question. Has not spoken since. The compound remains empty by quiet local agreement.
personality: mostly silent across decades but not by inclination
moves the ground in patterns that some scholars insist are deliberate
responds to certain offerings of buried things
remembered as 'kindly' by descendants of the warned workers
inattentive to most travelers entirely

The Bound One.name: The Bound One
The Bound One.basicInfo: Ancient, confined to a specific ruin longer than the oldest Draklid patriarch can remember. Drains nothing. Has not spoken in a century. Carries an unopened sealed letter addressed to the seventh Witch Queen. The Primes have been waiting forty years.
personality: motionless for years at a time
polite when addressed in proper court forms
indifferent to most contemporary affairs
preoccupied by the letter and what it contains
treats Prime emissaries with formal warmth and none of the contempt he holds for newer Bloodthanes

The Old Cliff.name: The Old Cliff
The Old Cliff.basicInfo: Pre-Ensoulment Sun-Scoured Plateau hunter. Longer than six wagons end to end; scales partially calcified to stone in places. Lets road-traffic alone; watches off-road parties from above for hours and chooses, by criteria no one has identified, whether to descend.
personality: watchful in a way that unsettles even Draklid
old beyond rage
selectively attentive to certain travelers and not others
rumored to have spared a particular Draklid lineage for reasons unknown
moves only at intervals long enough to be missed by most observers

The Brood-Mother of the Deep Reach.name: The Brood-Mother of the Deep Reach
The Brood-Mother of the Deep Reach.basicInfo: What the Fernwarg packs route around. Pre-pack, possibly pre-Ensoulment. Three sanctioned hunts have turned back at the territory edge — twice without explanation, once with the lead hunter weeping and refusing to discuss it.
personality: ancient
territorial in a way that registers as weather rather than aggression
the Fernwarg call her in their own tongue 'what was here first'
indifferent to small parties and singular about which ones she does not let pass
rumored to have once let a child through unharmed
